title: RYR1 / desflurane
summary: Patients with the rs193922762 CC genotype may have a decreased, but not absent, risk for malignant hyperthermia based on this variant when treated with volatile anesthetics (desflurane, enflurane, halothane, isoflurane, methoxyflurane, sevoflurane) and/or succinylcholine as compared to patients with the CT or TT genotype. Other genetic or clinical factors may also influence the risk for malignant hyperthermia.
riskSummary: RYR1 variant associated with toxicity for desflurane
detail: Patients with the rs193922762 CC genotype may have a decreased, but not absent, risk for malignant hyperthermia based on this variant when treated with volatile anesthetics (desflurane, enflurane, halothane, isoflurane, methoxyflurane, sevoflurane) and/or succinylcholine as compared to patients with the CT or TT genotype. Other genetic or clinical factors may also influence the risk for malignant hyperthermia.
whyItMatters: PharmGKB curates pharmacogenomic evidence from the literature, with level 1A–2B annotations backed by expert review and published clinical studies.
notes: PharmGKB annotations reflect published literature and should not replace clinical pharmacogenomic testing.

title: RYR1 / desflurane
summary: Patients with the rs193922762 CT genotype may develop malignant hyperthermia when treated with volatile anesthetics (desflurane, enflurane, halothane, isoflurane, methoxyflurane, sevoflurane) and/or succinylcholine as compared to patients with the CC genotype.
